children.text: Now listen to how Iggy Azalea raps. Does she still sound Australian? Or does she sound different?

Eberhardt & Freeman (2015) looked into Iggy Azalea's rap style and found that she uses several linguistic features of AAVE that are otherwise absent from her spoken speech. This includes rates of copula deletion that surpass even rappers who are native speakers of AAVE! They concluded that what Iggy Azalea is doing here is not integrating the Hip-Hop Nation Language as we saw in our CL and Snow Tha Product examples. Rather, Iggy Azalea is specifically trying to perform in a voice that most U.S. Americans would associate with Black women. This allows her to capitalize on the popularity of rap music without having to confront the social problems befalling the Black body.
